meta.name: The Palisade War
meta.description: Frontier clans raid each other's stockades for plunder and dread; you run one clan's war-band, holdfast by holdfast.
meta.author: axm-arc team

attributes.name: Muscle
attributes.description: Raw breach power — the força that splits a gate, a shield line, or a stockade wall.

attributes.name: Cunning
attributes.description: Timing and deception — the read on a watch-change or a patrol gap that turns a raid quiet instead of bloody.

attributes.name: Bulwark
attributes.description: Defense and armor — what keeps a raider or a rampart standing under a counter-raid.

attributes.name: Craft
attributes.description: Siegecraft and logistics — the engineering that fires a granary, saps a wall, or keeps a stockade's own stores full.

progressionTiers.name: Chapter I: The Outlying Steads
progressionTiers.flavorText: The rival clan's outer holdings — a lone watchtower, a timber camp, a salt-cellar stead — sit lightly guarded and ripe for a first blooding. Easy plunder to build the war-band's name.

progressionTiers.name: Chapter II: The Ring Forts
progressionTiers.flavorText: Word of the raids has spread, and the rival clan has dug in: a twin-gate fort, a hillcrest ring, a flooded moat holdfast. Every stockade now answers back.

progressionTiers.name: Chapter III: The Rival Seat
progressionTiers.flavorText: One outer palisade stands between the war-band and the rival clan's own bastion. Break it, and the feud is decided at the rival seat itself.

challenges.name: The Lone Watchtower
challenges.description: A single watchtower on the rival clan's outer boundary, manned by a bored two-man watch. The war-band's first raid, meant to be over before the tower's own bell can ring.

mechanicChecks.name: Breach the Palisade
mechanicChecks.description: Force the tower's outer stakes before the watch can raise the alarm.

mechanicChecks.name: Time the Watch-Change
mechanicChecks.description: Every raider reads the tower's watch rotation for the gap between shifts.

success.narrative: The tower falls before its bell ever sounds. The war-band's first plunder is small, but the boundary is broken.

partial.narrative: The tower falls but a runner slips the perimeter. The boundary is broken, though the rival clan now knows a war-band is moving.

failure.narrative: The watch-change never opens. The raiders pull back before the bell can ring on them instead.

challenges.name: The Timber Camp
challenges.description: A logging camp feeds the rival clan's stockade repairs. Burn its stores and every wall the rivals rebuild this season goes up slower.

mechanicChecks.name: Fire the Granary
mechanicChecks.description: The Sapper rigs the timber stacks and the camp's grain store to burn together.

mechanicChecks.name: Hold the Withdrawal
mechanicChecks.description: The rest of the war-band covers the burn and pulls out before the camp's guards regroup.

success.narrative: The timber camp goes up in one long burn. Every stockade wall the rival clan repairs this season will go up slower for it.

partial.narrative: The stacks catch, but the grain store is saved. The camp still burns; the rivals still eat.

failure.narrative: The rigging is found too soon. The camp's guards drive the war-band off before a spark ever catches.

challenges.name: The Salt-Cellar Stead
challenges.description: A stead built over the rival clan's salt cellar, the one store that keeps their whole war-band fed through a siege. Take it fast and the feud tilts before it properly starts.

mechanicChecks.name: Steady the Stores
mechanicChecks.description: The Forager keeps the salt cellar's stores from spoiling or scattering under fire.

mechanicChecks.name: Hold the Withdrawal
mechanicChecks.description: The war-band pushes the stead's guard detail out without torching the salt cellar itself.

mechanicChecks.name: Time the Watch-Change
mechanicChecks.description: Every raider checks the stead's yard for the snares the guard detail likes to leave behind.

success.narrative: The salt cellar changes hands whole. The rival clan's war-band eats thinner rations from here on.

partial.narrative: The guard detail is driven off, but half the salt cellar is fouled in the retreat. The stead is yours regardless.

failure.narrative: The guard detail holds the stead. The rival clan eats well another season.

challenges.name: The Twin-Gate Fort
challenges.description: The rival clan dug in properly here: a fort with two gatehouses covering each other, and pickets watching the approach. This is the ring forts' front door.

mechanicChecks.name: Breach the Palisade
mechanicChecks.description: The war-band forces the near gate while the far gate's crossbowmen open up.

mechanicChecks.name: Marshal the Assault
mechanicChecks.description: The Raider holds the breach's timing so the war-band doesn't bunch between the two gates.

mechanicChecks.name: Time the Watch-Change
mechanicChecks.description: The Forager reads the second gate's watch-change so it can't reinforce the first in time.

success.narrative: The near gate falls before the far one can answer. The fort's crossbowmen lay down arms rather than hold a stockade with one gate already open.

partial.narrative: The near gate falls but the far gate's watch scatters into the ring forts beyond. The fort's yard is yours; the rest will answer harder.

failure.narrative: The far gate's crossbowmen hold the breach too long. The war-band withdraws under fire from both gates.

challenges.name: The Hillcrest Ring
challenges.description: A ring stockade on the high ground warns the rest of the rival clan of every war-band on the move. Take it before the next signal fire is lit, or the whole clan learns you're coming.

mechanicChecks.name: Hold the Withdrawal
mechanicChecks.description: The Warden holds the ring's collapsing palisade open long enough for the war-band to get inside.

mechanicChecks.name: Steady the Climb
mechanicChecks.description: The war-band climbs the exposed hillside switchbacks together, roped and slow, with nowhere to take cover.

success.narrative: The ring falls before the next signal fire can be lit. For the first time in the feud, the war-band moves unannounced.

partial.narrative: The ring falls, but not before a single signal fire catches. Something in the rival seat has already noticed.

failure.narrative: The climb costs too much time. The signal fire catches, and the whole rival clan hears it.

challenges.name: The Flooded Moat Holdfast
challenges.description: A holdfast ringed by a flooded moat, held by a garrison that knows the ground better than any raider ever will. The rival clan built it to be the ring forts' hardest answer.

mechanicChecks.name: Breach the Palisade
mechanicChecks.description: The war-band fords the moat and forces the holdfast's gate against defenders who know every crossing point in it.

mechanicChecks.name: Hold the Withdrawal
mechanicChecks.description: The Raider keeps the war-band together through the holdfast yard's crossfire.

mechanicChecks.name: Break the Moat Wardens
mechanicChecks.description: The Warden breaks the holdfast's own moat-wardens, the garrison's answer to any war-band that gets past the flood.

mechanicChecks.name: Steady the Stores
mechanicChecks.description: The Forager keeps the war-band's own supply upright through a fight with no clean ground to fall back to.

success.narrative: The holdfast's gate comes down from its own moat-wardens' grapnels. Its stores change hands one last time.

partial.narrative: The holdfast falls, but its garrison captain slips out through a sluice gate no war-band had charted.

failure.narrative: The moat wardens hold and the yard's crossfire is too much. The war-band falls back from the flooded walls.

challenges.name: The Outer Palisade
challenges.description: The last palisade before the rival clan's own seat, and its garrison has had a season to prepare for exactly this war-band. Everyone who raids the seat crosses here first.

mechanicChecks.name: Breach the Palisade
mechanicChecks.description: The war-band forces the outer palisade against a garrison that has done nothing but drill for this raid.

mechanicChecks.name: Marshal the Assault
mechanicChecks.description: The Raider keeps the war-band's shape as the palisade's approach narrows to a killing lane.

mechanicChecks.name: Turn the Flank
mechanicChecks.description: The Forager scouts the scree slope to hit the garrison's rear before the lane closes.

mechanicChecks.name: Weather the Barrage
mechanicChecks.description: Every raider in the war-band endures the palisade's arrow and stone barrage as it closes the distance.

success.narrative: The outer palisade comes down, and with it the last thing standing between the war-band and the rival seat. The road to the bastion is open.

partial.narrative: The palisade falls, but the garrison's captain escapes toward the seat with warning. The rival bastion knows the war-band is coming now.

failure.narrative: The killing lane holds. The war-band pulls back from the outer palisade to lick its wounds and try again.

challenges.name: The Rival Bastion
challenges.description: The rival clan's own seat, and the warleader who ordered every counter-raid against you in the first place. There is nowhere left for either war-band to retreat to — this is where the feud is decided, one way or the other.

mechanicChecks.name: Breach the Palisade
mechanicChecks.description: The last gate in the rival seat, and its warleader's household guard has been told there is no yielding.

mechanicChecks.name: Marshal the Assault
mechanicChecks.description: The Raider carries the war-band through the bastion yard against the warleader's household guard.

mechanicChecks.name: Fire the Granary
mechanicChecks.description: The Sapper fires the bastion's own granary and stores so there is nothing left to hold a siege on.

mechanicChecks.name: Time the Watch-Change
mechanicChecks.description: Every raider reads the bastion yard for the warleader's last tricks before the final push.

success.narrative: The rival bastion falls, and with it the feud. The frontier answers to one war-band now — the one that broke every holdfast to get here.

partial.narrative: The bastion yard falls, but the warleader is gone by the time the war-band reaches the hall. The seat is yours; the warleader is someone else's feud now.

failure.narrative: The household guard holds the hall. The war-band withdraws from the rival seat, the feud not yet finished.

items.name: Watchman's Bronze Horn
items.flavorText: A brass horn looted off a watch that never got to blow it.

items.name: Timber-Raider's Hookblade
items.flavorText: Balanced for hooking timber stacks off a burn pile, rival ash still in the grain.

items.name: Salt-Cellar Ration Seal
items.flavorText: Stamped wax that once rationed a stead's salt; now it rations nothing but respect.

items.name: Twin-Gate Key-Bar
items.flavorText: Pried from the twin gate's own lock, and it still fits every rival stockade the war-band has cracked since.

items.name: Hillcrest Ring-Mail
items.flavorText: Riveted for a ring stockade garrison that never expected to need it this badly.

items.name: Moat-Warden's Grapnel
items.flavorText: Its hook still catches the old crossing lines, if anyone left remembers where they run.

items.name: Outer Palisade Standard
items.flavorText: Fought over so many times at the outer palisade that the sigil on it is unrecognizable.

items.name: Warleader's Bastion-Crown
items.flavorText: Plain iron, dented twice, and the only crown either clan on this frontier ever wanted back.

narrativeEvents.title: The First Torch
narrativeEvents.text: The lone watchtower falls without the rival clan ever learning it happened. For the first time since the feud began, a war-band is raiding inside their boundary — and somewhere on the ring forts, a counter-raid is already being called.

narrativeEvents.title: The Steads Answer
narrativeEvents.text: The outer steads are stripped bare, and the rival clan finally rides out in answer — a counter-raid on the war-band's own stockade, turned back at the gate. The feud is no longer one-sided.

narrativeEvents.title: The Rings Fall Silent
narrativeEvents.text: Twin-gate fort, hillcrest ring, and flooded moat holdfast — all cleared. The rival clan's ring forts are gone, and only the outer palisade stands between the war-band and their seat.

narrativeEvents.title: The Palisade Cracks
narrativeEvents.text: The outer palisade comes down after a season of the garrison's preparation. For the first time since the feud began, the road to the rival bastion lies open.

narrativeEvents.title: The War Ends
narrativeEvents.text: The rival bastion has fallen. The frontier is one clan's now, holdfast by holdfast, and the war-band that did it is written into its dread.
